item.description:
### Changed - If Codex isn't running Perch's hooks, the menu-bar dropdown now says so. Previously this only appeared in Settings, so you had to go looking for a problem you had no reason to suspect — the integration would read as on while doing nothing. Click the banner for the details and the fix. - Perch re-checks with Codex directly after an update. Approving a hook in Codex ties the approval to that exact hook, so a Perch update can quietly invalidate it; this is the case Perch couldn't previously detect on its own. - First-run welcome now mentions Codex when you have it, and walks Codex users through the one step Perch can't do for them: running `/hooks` to approve the hooks it just installed.
### Fixed - Claude Code sessions running inside the Claude desktop app were labelled `CLI`. The desktop app runs its own copy of Claude Code, and Perch couldn't tell it apart from one you started in a terminal. They now read `APP`, and clicking one brings the desktop app to the front instead of opening the project folder in Finder.
